heading: Confirmation of plan
label: 1191 Confirmation of plan
notes: Amendments2022—Subsec. (c)(3). added par. (3) and struck out former par. (3) which read as follows:Pub. L. 117–151“(3)(A)(i) The debtor will be able to make all payments under the plan; or“(ii) there is a reasonable likelihood that the debtor will be able to make all payments under the plan; and“(B) the plan provides appropriate remedies, which may include the liquidation of nonexempt assets, to protect the holders of claims or interests in the event that the payments are not made.”2020—Subsec. (f). , contingent on its addition by , struck out subsec. (f). Text read as follows: “Notwithstanding and subsection (e) of this section, a plan that provides for payment of a claim of a kind specified in may be confirmed under subsection (b) of this section if the plan proposes to make payments on account of such claim when due under the terms of the loan giving rise to such claim.”Pub. children.heading: .—Terms
children.label: a .—Terms
children.text: The court shall confirm a plan under this subchapter only if all of the requirements of section 1129(a), other than paragraph (15) of that section, of this title are met.1So in original. The words “of this title” probably should follow “1129(a)”.1

children.heading: .—Exception
children.label: b .—Exception
children.text: Notwithstanding , if all of the applicable requirements of , other than paragraphs (8), (10), and (15) of that section, are met with respect to a plan, the court, on request of the debtor, shall confirm the plan notwithstanding the requirements of such paragraphs if the plan does not discriminate unfairly, and is fair and equitable, with respect to each class of claims or interests that is impaired under, and has not accepted, the plan.section 510(a) of this titlesection 1129(a) of this title
With respect to a class of secured claims, the plan meets the requirements of .section 1129(b)(2)(A) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: the plan provides that all of the projected disposable income of the debtor to be received in the 3-year period, or such longer period not to exceed 5 years as the court may fix, beginning on the date that the first payment is due under the plan will be applied to make payments under the plan; or

children.label: B
children.text: the value of the property to be distributed under the plan in the 3-year period, or such longer period not to exceed 5 years as the court may fix, beginning on the date on which the first distribution is due under the plan is not less than the projected disposable income of the debtor.

children.label: A
children.text: The debtor will be able to make all payments under the plan; or

children.label: i
children.text: there is a reasonable likelihood that the debtor will be able to make all payments under the plan; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the plan provides appropriate remedies, which may include the liquidation of nonexempt assets, to protect the holders of claims or interests in the event that the payments are not made.

children.label: A
children.text: the maintenance or support of the debtor or a dependent of the debtor; or

children.label: B
children.text: a domestic support obligation that first becomes payable after the date of the filing of the petition; or
for the payment of expenditures necessary for the continuation, preservation, or operation of the business of the debtor.

children.heading: .—Special Rule
children.label: e .—Special Rule
children.text: Notwithstanding , a plan that provides for the payment through the plan of a claim of a kind specified in paragraph (2) or (3) of may be confirmed under subsection (b) of this section.section 1129(a)(9)(A) of this titlesection 507(a) of this title
